title: lobelia michelii var. subacaulis
intro: Lobelia michelii var. subacaulis, commonly known as Lobelia or Indian Tobacco, is a plant species in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae) that is native to the mountains of Central and Eastern Europe, and the Himalayas. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Lobelia michelii var. subacaulis include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as its potential to help with anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of this plant include smoking it as a tobacco substitute, making tea from the dried leaves, and using it in tinctures and salves for topical application.
